title: Volunteer fatigue shift rotation
summary: A safe rotation for volunteers helping with water, information, cleanup, queues, welfare checks, or transport so exhaustion does not create new emergencies.
content:
A safe rotation for volunteers helping with water, information, cleanup, queues, welfare checks, or transport so exhaustion does not create new emergencies.

Use it when the situation is real but still manageable. Work slowly, keep one written decision log, and prefer the safest reversible action over a dramatic improvisation.

Core sequence: 1) Set short shifts before people become exhausted, not after mistakes begin. 2) Pair new volunteers with someone who knows the task and the stop rule. 3) Record injuries, heat symptoms, anger, skipped meals, and repeated mistakes as reasons to rotate. 4) End the shift with a handover note instead of leaving the next person to guess.

Stop point: Do not use volunteer fatigue shift rotation to enter a restricted, contaminated, structurally unsafe, or officially closed area. Main warnings: Do not continue volunteer fatigue shift rotation after the stop signs named in the steps appear. Do not let urgency turn volunteer fatigue shift rotation into a task done alone without a visible check-in.
materials: shift board with start and stop times
water and rest point away from the task
buddy who can stop unsafe effort
steps: Set short shifts before people become exhausted, not after mistakes begin.
Pair new volunteers with someone who knows the task and the stop rule.
Record injuries, heat symptoms, anger, skipped meals, and repeated mistakes as reasons to rotate.
End the shift with a handover note instead of leaving the next person to guess.
warnings: Do not continue volunteer fatigue shift rotation after the stop signs named in the steps appear.
Do not let urgency turn volunteer fatigue shift rotation into a task done alone without a visible check-in.
common_mistakes: Leaving volunteer fatigue shift rotation undocumented makes the next person repeat the same risk.
Ignoring the first small warning during volunteer fatigue shift rotation often creates a larger recovery problem.
alternatives: If the normal method for volunteer fatigue shift rotation is blocked, do the smallest safe observation and return to a known safe point.
Ask a second person to read back the decision when stress or darkness affects volunteer fatigue shift rotation.
failure_signs: There is no named owner for volunteer fatigue shift rotation after the first action.
The environment changes faster than the group can record the result of volunteer fatigue shift rotation.
when_not_to_use: Do not use volunteer fatigue shift rotation to enter a restricted, contaminated, structurally unsafe, or officially closed area.
Do not continue volunteer fatigue shift rotation if it delays urgent medical care or an emergency call.
short_term: Complete one documented cycle of volunteer fatigue shift rotation and confirm that the next person can repeat it safely.
long_term: Review volunteer fatigue shift rotation after each real disruption and update the route, storage, contact, or stop rule that failed.
if_method_fails: If volunteer fatigue shift rotation fails, retreat to the last safe state, write the reason, and choose a smaller action with a named owner.
environment_notes: Adjust volunteer fatigue shift rotation for heat, rain, smoke, darkness, crowds, language barriers, limited mobility, and unreliable power.
